creator: michaelgalassi@gmail.com
text:
The man page for wait includes the sentence:

For wait(), wait3(), and wait4() functions, the single wpid argument specifies the set of child processes for which to wait.

wait() and wait3() however do not take a wpid argument.

Fix: 

I am reasonably certain that this sentence was meant to read:
was meant to refer to waitpid() and wait4() only.

For waitpid() and wait4() functions, the wpid argument specifies the set of child processes for which to wait.
How-To-Repeat: man 2 wait
